title: pera aperta
intro: Pera aperta, also known as Pericarpium Maydis or Corn Silk, is the dried stigmas of the maize plant (Zea mays). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating coughs, fever, and rheumatism. The health benefits of Pera aperta include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe respiratory issues and skin irritations. In herbal preparations, Pera aperta is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
